# List of Campaigns - URL: https://knowledgebase.webengage.com/docs/whatsapp-homepage - Publication Date: Not found - Scraped At: 2026-06-04T09:16:15.994414+00:00 List of Campaigns Understanding the central hub of WhatsApp in your dashboard List of Campaigns is the first interface you'll experience when you select WhatsApp from the navigation panel of your dashboard, as shown below. It's a central repository of all the WhatsApp Campaigns created by you, making it easy for you to create, analyze and modify campaigns. Click to enlarge Understanding List of Campaigns This section gives you an overview of your WhatsApp campaigns. You can apply campaign filters, search for the campaign using the campaign name in the search bar, and download and schedule the reports. Click to enlarge Campaign Filters You can search the campaigns by applying the campaign filters to get accurate result. Type As discussed under Campaigns and Its Types , messages sent through the various channels of engagement are called campaigns and can be of the following types: One-Time, Triggered, Recurring, Transactional, Journey, and Relay. While the default view shows you All types of campaigns, as shown above, you can customize the list by selecting a campaign type from the drop-down menu. Detailed read on Campaign and Its Types . Click to enlarge Status Each WhatsApp campaign can have any of the following statuses, discussed in further detail here : Draft Upcoming Running Paused Ended Click to enlarge Tags Tag is a handy feature that helps you categorize your campaigns as per their purpose, target audience, or any other parameter that makes them easier to locate. If you have added Tags while creating the campaign, they will show in the drop-down, making your search easier. Click to enlarge Search Campaign Skip the hunt - use the search bar placed on the top right to dig out existing campaigns.

have added Tags while creating the campaign, they will show in the drop-down, making your search easier. Click to enlarge Search Campaign Skip the hunt - use the search bar placed on the top right to dig out existing campaigns. Type in a keyword, we'll match it to all the campaign titles and tags, presenting a shortlist you can choose from! If you are looking for a particular campaign, then searching it by its name will fetch you the result right away. Click to enlarge Download and Schedule Reports 🚧 Please Note Reports can be downloaded and scheduled by only those Admins that have access to Account Management. If you're unable to do so, then please get in touch with your account owner. Here's how you can obtain a report to analyze the performance of multiple WhatsApp campaigns over a specific time frame: Click to enlarge Downloading a Report: As highlighted above, click the Download icon placed on the top right to obtain a copy of performance stats for all your WhatsApp campaigns for a specific time frame. Here's a step-by-step guide. Scheduling a Report: As highlighted above, click the Schedule icon placed on the top right to deliver performance reports periodically to your and your teammates' inbox. Here's a step-by-step guide. Visualization Format While the default data visualization format is a Percentage (% ), you can change this to a Numbers (#) through the overflow menu placed on the top right, as shown below. Click to enlarge Column Walkthrough Campaign features allow you to create WhatsApp Campaign , Modify Campaigns , and Analyze them. It also provides you a bird's eye view of the campaigns and their performance. Click to enlarge Let's walk you through all the column headers shown here: Campaign Name: Includes a list of all the

Campaigns , and Analyze them. It also provides you a bird's eye view of the campaigns and their performance. Click to enlarge Let's walk you through all the column headers shown here: Campaign Name: Includes a list of all the campaign titles hyperlinked to the campaign's analysis section. Type: Shown only when All is selected in the navigation bar, it indicates the type of campaign (One-time, Triggered, Recurring, Transactional, Journey). Detailed read Status: Each WhatsApp campaign can have any of the following statuses, discussed in further detail here . Start Date: Indicates the date and time the campaign started getting delivered to its target audience. Delivered: Indicates the total number of messages that have been delivered to the campaign's target audience over its entire lifetime. (Unique) Read: Calculated against Delivered messages, it indicates the percentage of users that have viewed or 'read' the WhatsApp message at least once. (Unique) Clicks: Calculated against Delivered messages, it indicates the percentage of users that have clicked on a link included within the message. (Unique) Conversions: Calculated against Delivered messages, it indicates the percentage of users that have performed the campaign's/ journey's Conversion Event within its Conversion Deadline . Detailed read on Conversion Tracking . Revenue: Indicates the amount of revenue generated by the total number of times users have performed the campaign's Conversion Event within its Conversion Deadline . Revenue is always shown in the Currency selected by you while setting up Revenue Mapping. Detailed read on Revenue Tracking . 🚧 Related Reads A comprehensive list of all the performance indicators tracked for WhatsApp Campaigns Action Centre Using the options nested under the overflow menu placed next to a Campaign's Name, you can choose to Edit, Delete, Duplicate or Tag a campaign, as shown below. You can also choose to Download/Schedule a performance

WhatsApp Campaigns Action Centre Using the options nested under the overflow menu placed next to a Campaign's Name, you can choose to Edit, Delete, Duplicate or Tag a campaign, as shown below. You can also choose to Download/Schedule a performance report for a specific campaign. Click to enlarge 📘 Please Note Journey campaigns can be edited only through the respective journey's Live View. For the same reason, you can only choose to Download/Schedule a performance report for journey campaigns. Please feel free to drop in a few lines at [email protected] in case you have any further queries or feedback. We're always just an email away! Updated 7 months ago So, what's next? # WhatsApp Template Media: Sizing Guide - URL: https://knowledgebase.webengage.com/docs/whatsapp-template-media-sizing-guide - Publication Date: Not found - Scraped At: 2026-06-04T09:16:15.994414+00:00 WhatsApp Template Media: Sizing Guide Documenting the sizing guide for your WhatsApp templates. When sending messages through the WhatsApp templates, it's a good idea to attach an image to the header of your template message. Adding an image can effectively capture your customers' attention and visually communicate your product, proposal, or offer. But what are the optimal dimensions for an image sent via WhatsApp templates? While you can technically send images with any aspect ratio, such as 1:1 or 16:9, and they will be delivered successfully, but there's a catch. In some cases, customers might need to tap on the image to view it in fullscreen to see the entire image clearly. Below are the updated media size requirements for your WhatsApp Templates: Media Type Format Maximum Size Image JPEG, PNG 5 MB Text Plain text, emojis, Markdown (limited) 4,096 characters Video 3GPP, MP4 Video (H.264 video codec and AAC audio codec) 16 MB Audio .aac , .m4a , . amr , .mp3 , .ogg 16 MB Sticker .webp (static/animated) 100 KB (static) 500 KB (animated) Location Latitude, longitude, optional name/address Document Any valid MIME-type 100 MB Contacts vCard object (name, phone, email, etc.) Updated 7 months ago Preface Copy Page

# WhatsApp - URL: https://knowledgebase.webengage.com/docs/whatsapp - Publication Date: Not found - Scraped At: 2026-06-04T09:16:15.994414+00:00 WhatsApp Welcome to WebEngage's Guide to getting started with WhatsApp Business Messaging According to a survey by AdWeek , 68% of customers think that messaging is the most convenient way to connect with a business. And what's a better way to message than on WhatsApp? Leverage the power of WebEngage to forge lasting connections with users on a channel they value the most - WhatsApp. The Power of WhatsApp To put things into perspective, here's why this new channel deserves to be an integral part of your engagement strategy: 1. Most Preferred Channel of Engagement: With over 1.5 billion users worldwide and 60 billion messages sent per day , WhatsApp has revolutionized the way we communicate. This makes it the perfect platform to connect with users. 2. Interactive Communication: Modern customers want to interact with a business just like they communicate with friends and family. WhatsApp enables you to provide the same comfort with secured, informal chats that feel just like face-to-face conversations. 3. Unparalleled Brand Recognition: You can easily leverage WhatsApp to reinforce your brand identity by creating a stunning business profile and providing company details like phone number, address, website/social media links. Coupled with contextual campaigns and thoughtful conversations, you can make your business stand out! 4. Unrestricted Accessibility: WhatsApp provides personal and easy access to a user's life. It opens up a two-way channel of communication that enables users and businesses alike to connect whenever needed.
